Italy Halts Military Cooperation With Israel Over Middle East Wars PM Giorgia Meloni announced Italy suspended automatic renewal of its defence cooperation agreement with Israel, siting Middle East conflicts. Military training cooperation is now halted, deepening a rift that began with arms export restrictions in October 2023. READ MORE

🇷🇸 🇮🇱 ⚡️ Serbia and Israel to Jointly Produce Combat Drones, Vučić Announces “We will be doing it together… 50-50… and we will have the best drones in this part of the world,” Vučić said during a visit to a military unit. He added that Serbia alone “cannot make drones like Israel.” The project involves Israel’s Elbit Systems (holding a 51% stake) and Serbia’s state-owned Yugoimport-SDPR (49%).

Status-6 (War & Military News) Last night, Ukrainian Air Force carried out strikes on the storage, preparation and launch sites for Russian Shahed/Geran-type long-range OWA-UAVs located on the territory of the Donetsk International Airport in Russia-occupied part of eastern Ukraine. The strikes were reportedly carried out with French-supplied SCALP-EG long-range cruise missiles and US-supplied GBU-39/B SDB guided aerial bombs. The attack was monitored in real-time by a Ukrainian UAV. tweet

Open Source Intel French President, Macron: Yesterday, I spoke with Iranian President Massoud Pezeshkian, as well as with U.S. President Donald Trump. I urged the resumption of the negotiations suspended in Islamabad, the clearing up of misunderstandings, and the avoidance of any further escalation. It is essential, in particular, that the ceasefire be strictly respected by all parties and that it include Lebanon. It is equally important that the Strait of Hormuz be reopened unconditionally, without restrictions or tolls, as soon as possible. Under these conditions, negotiations should be able to resume quickly, with the support of the key parties concerned. France and the United Kingdom will also host a conference in Paris this Friday, bringing together by videoconference non-belligerent countries ready to contribute, alongside us, to a multilateral and purely defensive mission aimed at restoring freedom of navigation in the strait when security conditions allow. tweet

↩️ Reply to OSINT OSINTdefender RT @DefenseBulletin : China has increased coercive deterrence measures against U.S.-aligned nations, specifically the Philippines, in the region and U.S. overflight permissions would almost certainly draw ire from China, possibly inviting Chinese coercive measures, such as ADIZ violations, maritime coercion, and economic coercive measures, on Indonesia. The operational benefits of “notification only” overflight rights for U.S. military aircraft over the strategic air and maritime corridor would directly potentiate any U.S. response to a Taiwan invasion and other potential Chinese aggression in the Indo-pacific. According to Reuters, Indonesian Foreign Ministry sent a letter to the Indonesian Defense Ministry warning against granting blanket flyover permission to U.S. military flights, citing the risk of friction with China in the South China Sea region. The letter was delivered prior to the meeting between U.S. Secretary of War Pete Hegseth and Indonesian Defense Minister Sjafrie Sjamsoeddin in Washington yesterday. - OSINTdefender tweet

Visioner 🇫🇷 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 🇱🇧 🇬🇧 French President Emmanuel Macron said He spoke with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian and U.S. President Donald Trump, calling for the resumption of negotiations that broke down in Islamabad and for the ceasefire to be strictly respected by all, including Lebanon. He urged the unconditional reopening of the Strait of Hormuz without controls or tolls. He stated that France and the United Kingdom will host a videoconference on Friday in Paris with non‑belligerent countries ready to contribute to a multilateral defensive mission to restore freedom of navigation in the strait once security conditions allow. tweet

⚡ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 David Barnea the head of Israel’s Mossad delivered a statement warning of ongoing existential threats, particularly from Iran, and outlining Israel’s strategic posture following recent operations: “The naïve are mistaken in thinking the Holocaust belongs to the past, and that in today’s reality genocide, calls for annihilation, or hatred threatening a people’s existence cannot occur. The Iranian threat has grown before our eyes, almost unchecked by the world. We repeatedly warned of the nuclear threat as an existential danger, as well as the growing arsenal of ballistic missiles aimed at Israeli civilians. Ultimately, we took our fate into our own hands and launched two necessary wars. In the ‘Roaring Lion’ war, the IDF, with support from the Mossad, carried out an unprecedented campaign against the Iranian regime — one that openly calls for our destruction. Alongside strong allies, we fought in what was described as a historic cooperation for shared values of justice and freedom. After 40 days of intense fighting, significant achievements were made, including damage to the enemy’s central objective of destroying Israel. However, the mission is not yet complete. The campaign, it was stated, will continue beyond the direct strikes. Our commitment will only be fulfilled when this extremist regime is replaced. A regime that seeks our destruction cannot remain. This is our mission — we will not stand by in the face of another existential threat. ‘Never again.’” @wfwitness
🛰️ Satellite images show a U.S. CH-47 Chinook helicopter hit by Iranian strikes at Camp Buehring in Kuwait, and a CH-53 Sea Stallion helicopter damaged at Ali Al Salem Air Base.

⚡ 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 Finance Minister Yassine Jaber, a Shia figure affiliated with the Amal Movement, told Al Jadeed that today’s meeting in Washington is key to reaching a ceasefire, emphasizing that halting the fighting remains the top priority. His remarks come despite Hezbollah and the Amal-Hezbollah alliance’s broader stance rejecting talks with Israel, marking a notable divergence in tone. @wfwitness

This type of weapon is designed to replace the old ATACMS systems and offers greater range and accuracy.

Open Source Intel “Bechadarei” reports Ultra Orthodox analysts played a key intelligence role in the IDF strike that eliminated ~250 Hezbollah commanders and operatives in 10 minutes in Lebanon. Graduates of the “Pardes” program, which trains Ultra Orthodox men in intelligence analysis and advanced data processing, they helped process months of accumulated and real time intelligence. Their work enabled precise tracking of dozens of targets and a highly coordinated, near simultaneous strike. Image: Avratech director Aaron Safrai (right) with Ultra Orthodox students learning data processing and programming at the company’s Jerusalem offices. (Photo: Marc Israel Sellem) tweet
